Note: SQLite does not support FULL OUTER JOIN natively. If you need a true FULL OUTER JOIN equivalent, use:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM posts
LEFT JOIN comments ON posts.id = comments.post_id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
UNION
SELECT *
FROM posts
RIGHT JOIN comments ON posts.id = comments.post_id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
```

```sql
  u1.is_verified AS follower_is_verified,
```

input_prompt: SELECT all columns FROM likes FULL JOIN posts WHERE p7.content = 'user'
gold_sql: SELECT * FROM likes AS l1 FULL JOIN posts AS p7 ON l1.post_id = p7.id WHERE p7.content = 'user'
generated_response:
```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
FULL OUTER JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
```

Note: SQLite does not support FULL OUTER JOIN natively. Here's the SQLite-compatible version:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
LEFT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
UNION
SELECT *
FROM likes
RIGHT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
```

Or more simply, if you want all matching rows:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
```


```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
FULL OUTER JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
```

Note: SQLite does not support FULL OUTER JOIN natively. If you need to run this on SQLite, use:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
LEFT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
UNION
SELECT *
FROM likes
RIGHT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
```

Or the SQLite-compatible version:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
LEFT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
UNION ALL
SELECT *
FROM posts
LEFT JOIN likes ON posts.id = likes.post_id
WHERE posts.content = 'user' AND likes.user_id IS NULL
```

```sql
  u2.id AS followee_id_user_id,
  u2.username AS followee_username,
  u2.email AS followee_email,
  u2.signup_date AS followee_signup_date,
  u2.is_verified AS followee_is_verified,
  u2.country_code AS followee_country_code
FROM follows f
LEFT JOIN users u1 ON f.follower_id = u1.id
LEFT JOIN users u2 ON f.followee_id = u2.id;
```

```sql

WHERE posts.content = 'user'
```

Note: SQLite does not support FULL JOIN natively. If you need to run this on SQLite, use:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
LEFT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
UNION
SELECT *
FROM likes
RIGHT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
```

Or the SQLite-compatible version:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
LEFT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
UNION ALL
SELECT *
FROM likes
RIGHT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE posts.content = 'user'
AND likes.user_id IS NULL
```

Note: SQLite does not support FULL JOIN natively. If you need a true FULL JOIN equivalent, use:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
LEFT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id

UNION
SELECT *
FROM likes
RIGHT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE likes.user_id < 606
```

However, if the intent is a standard LEFT JOIN (which is more common), use:

```sql

FROM likes
```


input_prompt: SELECT all columns FROM likes FULL JOIN posts on post_id equals id WHERE user_id less than 606
gold_sql: SELECT * FROM likes AS l1 FULL JOIN posts AS p4 ON l1.post_id = p4.id WHERE p4.user_id < 606
generated_response:
```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
FULL OUTER JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE likes.user_id < 606
```

Note: SQLite does not support FULL OUTER JOIN natively. If you need SQLite compatibility, use:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
LEFT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE likes.user_id < 606
UNION
SELECT *
FROM likes
RIGHT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE likes.user_id < 606
```

Or the SQLite-compatible version:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM likes
LEFT JOIN posts ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE likes.user_id < 606
UNION ALL
SELECT *
FROM posts
LEFT JOIN likes ON likes.post_id = posts.id
WHERE likes.user_id < 606 AND likes.user_id IS NULL
```

gold_sql: SELECT * FROM comments AS c1 FULL JOIN users AS u7 ON c1.user_id = u7.id WHERE u7.is_verified = 1
generated_response:
```sql
SELECT *
FROM comments
FULL JOIN users ON comments.user_id = users.id
WHERE users.is_verified = 1;
```

Note: SQLite does not support FULL JOIN natively. If you need to use this query in SQLite, use:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM comments
LEFT JOIN users ON comments.user_id = users.id
WHERE users.is_verified = 1
UNION
SELECT *
FROM comments
RIGHT JOIN users ON comments.user_id = users.id
WHERE users.is_verified = 1;
```

However, if the intent is simply a join with the filter, the standard approach for SQLite would be:

```sql
SELECT *
FROM comments
INNER JOIN users ON comments.user_id = users.id
WHERE users.is_verified = 1;
```
